task_completion.description: Verify that the initial control points (Point 1 and Point 2) are loaded or entered into TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The TopoCal interface shows Point 1 (E=481500.250, N=4398200.500) and Point 2 (E=481500.250, N=4398700.500) in the point list, coordinate table, or map view.

task_completion.description: Verify that the COGO/Radiation tool is configured with the correct Occupied Station and Backsight.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A COGO or Radiation dialog is open in TopoCal with Point 1 set as the Occupied Station (or base) and Point 2 set as the Reference/Backsight with an azimuth of 0 degrees.

task_completion.description: Verify the computation of Point 101 using the specified azimuth and distance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The COGO tool shows inputs for Point 101 (Azimuth 45°30'00", Distance 150.000 m), or the TopoCal point list displays Point 101 with coordinates approximately E=481607.2, N=4398305.6.

task_completion.description: Verify the computation of Point 102 using the specified azimuth and distance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The COGO tool shows inputs for Point 102 (Azimuth 135°15'30", Distance 210.500 m), or the TopoCal point list displays Point 102 with coordinates approximately E=481648.4, N=4398050.1.

task_completion.description: Verify the computation of Point 103 using the specified azimuth and distance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The COGO tool shows inputs for Point 103 (Azimuth 270°00'00", Distance 300.250 m), or the TopoCal point list displays Point 103 with coordinates approximately E=481200.0, N=4398200.5.

task_completion.description: Verify that the TopoCal project is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or the TopoCal application title bar shows the project being saved as 'cogo_project.top' in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\TopoCal' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the updated point list is exported as a CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog, success message, or file explorer window shows 'computed_points.csv' being saved in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\TopoCal' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the TopoCal application GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the TopoCal software interface, not just terminal commands or Python scripts calculating the coordinates.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the COGO/Radiation tools rather than manually typing the final coordinates.
integrity.visual_evidence: The COGO/Radiation tool dialogs should be visible with the azimuth and distance inputs being entered, rather than the agent directly typing the theoretical Easting/Northing values into a table.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply create the output CSV file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of Notepad or other text editors being used to manually type out the 'computed_points.csv' file contents.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected coordinates for the computed points (tolerance ±0.05m): Point 101 (E=481607.238, N=4398305.636), Point 102 (E=481648.423, N=4398050.984), Point 103 (E=481200.000, N=4398200.500). The project must be saved to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\TopoCal\cogo_project.top' and the points exported to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\TopoCal\computed_points.csv'.
